label: Compressed earth body
description: Anthropomorphic carved earth mass representing land under extractive pressure: cut, compressed, wounded, and made into structure rather than living relation.
notes: Core left-side figure. Must remain visible even in early state.

label: Relational living earth
description: Distributed living earth figure made from canopy, slope, water, rocks, understory, fungal life, animal presence, and cultivated relations.
notes: Not a single statue-like figure. It is a body-landscape.

label: River border
description: The river is the real border between the two worlds and the methodological seam of the app.

label: Black dog chained to tree
description: Black chained dog near the extractive land figure, representing captivity, coercion, and animal life held inside extractive relations.
notes: Confirmed detail in MAP 22. Do not remove.

label: Monoculture grid blocks
description: Regular grid-like planting blocks representing simplified land use, monoculture logic, and spatial flattening.
notes: Useful for yield, monoculture, subsidy and land-use entries.

label: Cavities and tunnels
description: Openings, tunnels, and cut sections in the extractive side showing depletion, rupture, and forced access to the land body.
notes: Can thicken with erosion, compaction, soil loss, mining, or overwork data.

label: Controlled tree mass
description: Dark branching tree-like form embedded in the extractive side, connected to control, shadow, restraint, and habitat loss.
notes: Can link to deforestation, hunting pressure, habitat fragmentation, and legal rulings.

label: Exposed eroded ground
zone: left lower slopes and exposed brown mass
description: Bare brown exposed ground representing soil loss, compaction, erosion, and degraded structure.
notes: Can react to tillage, erosion, runoff, drought and compaction entries.

label: Paulownia trees in flower
description: Purple flowering Paulownia trees. These are not generic flowers; they are flowering trees integrated into the regenerative landscape.
notes: Corrected detail: skip generic purple flowers. Use Paulownia in flower.

label: Canopy and understory guild
description: Layered vegetation expressing food forest structure, ecological succession, habitat continuity, and mixed-species relations.

label: Water retention and stream system
zone: right lower water and stream paths
description: Visible water movement, retention zones, stream branches, and hydrated landforms representing hydrological repair.
notes: Connect to drought resilience, runoff, infiltration, wetland, riparian and water-holding data.

label: Fungal and mushroom zone
zone: right lower understory and soil edge
description: Fungal / mushroom presence representing soil food web, decomposition, mycorrhizal relations, and living substrate.
notes: Can be enriched by soil microbiome, compost, mycorrhiza, and substrate entries.

label: Rocks and living soil
description: Rocks, organic matter, hydrated surface, and small soil structures forming the living base of the regenerative side.
notes: Should carry field data, soil tests, ECHO-style observations, and hydrological markers.

label: Integrated solar panels
description: Solar panels integrated into roofs, small structures, terrain-friendly arrays, and regenerative infrastructure rather than imposed industrial objects.
notes: Not present as a finalized MAP 22 base element yet; should be generated as a compatible layer.

label: Solar drones monitoring fire, air, and soil
description: Lightweight solar monitoring drones for wildfire risk, air quality, and soil/vegetation monitoring, belonging to the regenerative system.
notes: Generated layer should feel ecological, not military or industrial.

label: Community practice paths
zone: right pathways and cultivated relation areas
description: Paths, working spaces, care zones, nursery logic, and human-scale practice areas where evidence becomes lived technique.
notes: Can connect to gallery uploads, farm reports, forum threads, and guided learning.
